topics.topic: PRODUCTION OF IRON AND STEEL
Name the various raw materials required for production of iron
Describe the method of producing Pig Iron in Blast furnace
Describe the puddling furnace to produce wrought iron
Explain the process of manufacturing cast iron in Cupola
Describe the manufacturing of steel by Bessemer process, L.D. process, Open Hearth and Electric Process

PRODUCTION OF IRON AND STEEL Raw materials, iron ores, Lime stone , Manufacturing of pig iron from blast furnace. Wrought iron by pudding furnace. Cast Iron from cupola. Production of steel by Bessemer, L.D. process; Open hearth and Electric processes
HEAT TREATMENT OF STEEL
Explain the cooling curves of pure metal
Mention the allotropic forms of pure iron with temperatures and their crystal structures
Draw the iron carbon equilibrium diagram, identify various structures of the iron carbon system
State the importance of heat treatment for steels
Explain Annealing, Normalising, Hardening and Tempering
Explain use of case hardening processes like; carburizing, nitriding and Cyaniding

HEAT TREATMENT OF STEEL Cooling curve for pure metal. Allotropic forms of pure Iron. Iron carbon equilibrium diagram. Importance of heat treatment. Heat treatment processes – annealing, normalizing, hardening, tempering, carburizing, nitriding and cyaniding
MILLING MACHINES AND GEAR MAKING PROCESSES
Explain the working principle of a Milling machine
Explain the constructional details and Principle of operation of Column and knee type and Universal milling machines with legible sketches
State the purpose and list the methods of indexing
List different types of milling cutters and their applications
State the functions of different tool holding devices
State the functions of different work holding devices
Explain various milling operations
List specifications of milling machines
List key aspects of gear Nomeneclature
List methods of producing gears
Describe the method of gear manufacturing by Gear shaping
Describe the method of gear generation by Gear hobbing
Explain various gear finishing operations

MILLING MACHINES AND GEAR GENERATING PROCESSES Definition - Types of Milling machines: Constructional details and Principle of operation of Column and knee type and Universal milling machines - Specification of milling machines – Purpose of indexing – Methods of indexing - Work holding and Tool holdin g devices – Types of milling cutters – Cutter materials - Milling Operations :Face Milling, Plain Milling, Side Milling, Straddle Milling and Gang Milling. Definition of Gear – Gear nomenclature - Methods of manufacturing of gears – Gear generating processes: Principle of operation of Gear Shaping and Gear hobbing – Gear materials - Gear finishing processes: Burnishing, Shaving, Honing, Lapping and Super finishing
WELDING
NON CONVENTIONAL MACHINING PROCESSES
Distinguish between nonconventional machining processes and conventional methods
State the relative advantages and applications of nonconventional machining processes
Describe Ultrasonic machining (USM) Process with advantages and disadvantages
Describe Electrical Discharge Machining (EDM) Process with advantages and disadvantages
Describe of Abrasive jet machining (AJM) with advantages and disadvantages

NON CONVENTIONAL MACHINING PROCESSES Introduction – Comparison with Conventional machining processes – Methods of Nonconventional Machining Processes – Applications. Description of Ultrasonic machining (USM) Process - advantages – disadvantages. Description of Electrical Discharge Machining ( EDM) Process – advantages – disadvantages. Description of Abrasive jet machining - advantages – disadvantages. Description of Laser beam machining - advantages – disadvantages. REFERENCE BOOKS 1. Material Science and Engineering - Raghavan PHI Publishers 2. Introduction to Physical Metallurgy - Avner Tata McGraw Hill Publishers 3. Material science and metallurgy - O P Khanna Dhanpati Rai Publishers 4. Manufacturing Technology - P N Rao (MGH Publishers) 5. Production Technology - R.C.Patel 6. Production Technology - Jain & Gupta. Table specifying the scope of syllabus to be covered for Unit Test-I & Unit Test-II Unit Test Learning Outcomes to be covered Unit Test – I From 1.1 to 3.10 Unit Test – II From 3.11 to 5.6
